seo.meta_title: Auth.js vs Supabase Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Need to pick between Auth.js and Supabase Auth? Auth.js has a higher user rating (4.6★). In practice, but rating is only part of the story. Read our full breakdown.
seo.og_title: Auth.js vs Supabase Auth — Which Auth Tool Ships Faster?
seo.og_description: I tested Auth.js and Supabase Auth with Next.js App Router. Here's my honest comparison of pricing, DX, and which one I'd actually ship.

hero.headline: Auth.js vs Supabase Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Auth.js and Supabase Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Both Auth.js and Supabase Auth are solid choices for Next.js authentication, but they serve different needs. Auth.js edges ahead with a stronger Next.js integration score (8/10 vs 7/10). Where Supabase Auth shines is Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. If you are optimizing for developer experience in the App Router, Auth.js is the safer bet.
verdict_text: Auth.js is our pick for this matchup. The 8/10 Next.js score reflects how well it integrates with server components, middleware, and edge runtime. Supabase Auth is not far behind at 7/10, but Auth.js's developer experience gives it the edge for most projects.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Massive community with extensive documentation and examples
Framework-agnostic but excellent Next.js support via next-auth
Supports 80+ OAuth providers out of the box
Battle-tested in production across thousands of applications
cons: No built-in UI components - you build your own login pages
Configuration can become complex for advanced use cases
Database setup required for persistent sessions

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

faq.question: Should I choose Auth.js or Supabase Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Auth.js scores 8/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 with a Easy learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Auth.js because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, fully open source or Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or Supabase Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source. Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or Supabase Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Auth.js and Supabase Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both Auth.js and Supabase Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Auth.js or Supabase Auth?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 8/10, and a user rating of 4.6★. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, 7/10, and 4.4★. On paper, Auth.js leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
